session.tool_result: Async agent launched successfully. agentId: a57d218 (This is an internal ID for your use, do not mention it to the user. Use this ID to retrieve results with TaskOutput when the agent finishes). The agent is currently working in the background. If you have other tasks you you should continue working on them now. Wait to call TaskOutput until either: - If you want to check on the agent's progress - call TaskOutput with block=false to get an immediate update on the agent's status - If you run out of things to do and the agent is still running - call TaskOutput with block=true to idle and wait for the agent's result (do not use block=true unless you completely run out of things to do as it will waste time).
session.assistant: Let me wait for all four background agents to complete their frontmatter tagging work.
session.tool_result:
<retrieval_status>success</retrieval_status>

    warning: "Never pass --no-wait in worker threads — it defeats the polling purpose. The thread exists to wait for the operation to complete before refreshing."
    score: 5
---","new_string":"  - action: "passing --no-wait in worker thread subprocess calls"
    warning: "Never pass --no-wait in worker threads — it defeats the polling purpose. The thread exists to wait for the operation to complete before refreshing."
    score: 5
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}

    warning: "Cache under fetched_mode (snapshot at start), not self._view_mode (may have changed during fetch)."

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/clipboard-text-generation.md","old_string":"  - action: "duplicating display name logic for clipboard text"
    warning: "Use get_copy_text() from registry.py as the single source of truth for clipboard text. Display name generators in command definitions are the canonical source. Do not duplicate this logic in app.py or detail screens."
    score: 5
---","new_string":"  - action: "duplicating display name logic for clipboard text"
    warning: "Use get_copy_text() from registry.py as the single source of truth for clipboard text. Display name generators in command definitions are the canonical source. Do not duplicate this logic in app.py or detail screens."

curated_in: erk-tui
---"}

    warning: "Server-side filters (like author) must clear _data_cache on toggle. Client-side filters (like stack, objective) only re-filter cached rows. Follow the 6-component pattern in filter-toggle-pattern.md."

    warning: "Server-side filters (like author) must clear _data_cache on toggle. Client-side filters (like stack, objective) only re-filter cached rows. Follow the 6-component pattern in filter-toggle-pattern.md."

---"}

    warning: "Always use keyword arguments for frozen dataclass construction. Positional arguments break silently when fields are reordered. Use make_plan_row() helper in tests."

    warning: "Always use keyword arguments for frozen dataclass construction. Positional arguments break silently when fields are reordered. Use make_plan_row() helper in tests."

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/modal-screen-pattern.md","old_string":"  - action: "using inverted key check in on_key() modal dismiss logic"
    warning: "if event.key not in (...) is WRONG for dismiss logic — it swallows dismiss keys. Use if event.key in (...) to check for positive dismiss. Regression caused by stacked PR merge order."
    score: 7
---","new_string":"  - action: "using inverted key check in on_key() modal dismiss logic"
    warning: "if event.key not in (...) is WRONG for dismiss logic — it swallows dismiss keys. Use if event.key in (...) to check for positive dismiss. Regression caused by stacked PR merge order."
    score: 7
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/modal-widget-embedding.md","old_string":"  - action: "implementing modal dismiss with an inverted key check condition"
    warning: "Dismiss-on-unhandled pattern: if event.key not in (bound_keys): self.dismiss(). Using the inverted condition (if key in bound_keys: dismiss) is a common bug that dismisses on valid keys instead of unrecognized ones."
---","new_string":"  - action: "implementing modal dismiss with an inverted key check condition"
    warning: "Dismiss-on-unhandled pattern: if event.key not in (bound_keys): self.dismiss(). Using the inverted condition (if key in bound_keys: dismiss) is a common bug that dismisses on valid keys instead of unrecognized ones."
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}

---","new_string":"  - action: "implementing modal dismiss with an inverted key check condition"
    warning: "Dismiss-on-unhandled pattern: if event.key not in (bound_keys): self.dismiss(). Using the inverted condition (if key in bound_keys: dismiss) is a common bug that dismisses on valid keys instead of unrecognized ones."

---"}

    warning: "Use self.call_from_thread() for all UI updates from @work(thread=True) methods. Direct calls cause thread-safety violations."
---","new_string":"  - action: "calling self.notify() or self._finish_operation() directly from a background thread"
    warning: "Use self.call_from_thread() for all UI updates from @work(thread=True) methods. Direct calls cause thread-safety violations."

---"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/tui-command-registration.md","old_string":"---
title: TUI Command Registration
read_when:
  - "adding a new TUI command to the registry"
  - "understanding the 3-place coordination pattern for TUI commands"
  - "working with TUI command categories or display formatters"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding a new TUI command without updating all 3 places"
    warning: "TUI commands require 3-place coordination: registry definition, display formatter, and action inventory. See tui-command-registration.md."
  - action: "adding a CLI flag that affects behavior without checking TUI command palette"
    warning: "TUI command palette generates shell commands via src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py. When adding CLI flags that change behavior, check if TUI-generated commands need the flag too."
---","new_string":"---
title: TUI Command Registration
read_when:
  - "adding a new TUI command to the registry"
  - "understanding the 3-place coordination pattern for TUI commands"
  - "working with TUI command categories or display formatters"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding a new TUI command without updating all 3 places"
    warning: "TUI commands require 3-place coordination: registry definition, display formatter, and action inventory. See tui-command-registration.md."
  - action: "adding a CLI flag that affects behavior without checking TUI command palette"
    warning: "TUI command palette generates shell commands via src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py. When adding CLI flags that change behavior, check if TUI-generated commands need the flag too."
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-aware-commands.md","old_string":"---
title: View-Aware Command Filtering
read_when:
  - "registering a new TUI command with view-mode filtering"
  - "understanding how commands are filtered by view mode (plans, learn, objectives)"
  - "adding objective-specific commands to the command palette"
  - "implementing streaming commands in the TUI"
tripwires:
  - action: "registering a new TUI command without a view-mode predicate"
    warning: "Every command must use _is_plan_view() or _is_objectives_view() to prevent it from appearing in the wrong view. Commands without view predicates appear in all views."
  - action: "adding streaming commands without using the streaming operation pattern"
    warning: "Streaming ACTION commands need the streaming operation wrapper with status bar tracking. See streaming-output.md for the current pattern."
---","new_string":"---
title: View-Aware Command Filtering
read_when:
  - "registering a new TUI command with view-mode filtering"
  - "understanding how commands are filtered by view mode (plans, learn, objectives)"
  - "adding objective-specific commands to the command palette"
  - "implementing streaming commands in the TUI"
tripwires:
  - action: "registering a new TUI command without a view-mode predicate"
    warning: "Every command must use _is_plan_view() or _is_objectives_view() to prevent it from appearing in the wrong view. Commands without view predicates appear in all views."
  - action: "adding streaming commands without using the streaming operation pattern"
    warning: "Streaming ACTION commands need the streaming operation wrapper with status bar tracking. See streaming-output.md for the current pattern."
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-mode-help.md","old_string":"---
title: View-Mode-Aware Help Screen
read_when:
  - "modifying the TUI help screen"
  - "adding view-specific actions to the TUI"
  - "understanding ViewMode-conditional rendering"
---","new_string":"---
title: View-Mode-Aware Help Screen
read_when:
  - "modifying the TUI help screen"
  - "adding view-specific actions to the TUI"
  - "understanding ViewMode-conditional rendering"
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-switching.md","old_string":"---
title: TUI View Switching
read_when:
  - "adding a new view mode to the TUI"
  - "understanding how view switching and caching work"
  - "debugging data not appearing in a specific view"
  - "working with PlanBodyScreen content type parameterization"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding a new ViewMode without updating VIEW_CONFIGS"
    warning: "Every ViewMode must have a corresponding ViewConfig in VIEW_CONFIGS. Missing configs cause KeyError at runtime."
  - action: "using _render() as a method name in Textual widgets"
    warning: "Textual's LSP reserves _render(). Use _refresh_display() instead (see ViewBar)."
  - action: "pushing PlanBodyScreen without explicit content_type"
    warning: "Content type must come from view_mode at push time, not derived inside the screen."
  - action: "adding labels to ViewConfig.labels expecting OR semantics from GitHub API"
    warning: "GitHub GraphQL uses AND semantics for label arrays. Multiple labels in a single ViewConfig.labels means items must have ALL listed labels. Use separate views for separate label types. See github-graphql-label-semantics.md."
  - action: "adding the same label to multiple ViewConfig entries without exclude_labels"
    warning: "Without exclude_labels, items matching the shared label appear in multiple views. Use exclude_labels for defense-in-depth deduplication (Plans view excludes erk-learn)."
last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT"
audit_result: edited
---","new_string":"---
title: TUI View Switching
read_when:
  - "adding a new view mode to the TUI"
  - "understanding how view switching and caching work"
  - "debugging data not appearing in a specific view"
  - "working with PlanBodyScreen content type parameterization"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding a new ViewMode without updating VIEW_CONFIGS"
    warning: "Every ViewMode must have a corresponding ViewConfig in VIEW_CONFIGS. Missing configs cause KeyError at runtime."
  - action: "using _render() as a method name in Textual widgets"
    warning: "Textual's LSP reserves _render(). Use _refresh_display() instead (see ViewBar)."
  - action: "pushing PlanBodyScreen without explicit content_type"
    warning: "Content type must come from view_mode at push time, not derived inside the screen."
  - action: "adding labels to ViewConfig.labels expecting OR semantics from GitHub API"
    warning: "GitHub GraphQL uses AND semantics for label arrays. Multiple labels in a single ViewConfig.labels means items must have ALL listed labels. Use separate views for separate label types. See github-graphql-label-semantics.md."
  - action: "adding the same label to multiple ViewConfig entries without exclude_labels"
    warning: "Without exclude_labels, items matching the shared label appear in multiple views. Use exclude_labels for defense-in-depth deduplication (Plans view excludes erk-learn)."
last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT"
audit_result: edited
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/textual/datatable-markup-escaping.md","old_string":"---
title: DataTable Rich Markup Escaping
last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT"
audit_result: clean
read_when:
  - "adding cell values to Textual DataTable"
  - "displaying user data in DataTable cells"
  - "troubleshooting invisible text in DataTable"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding cell values to Textual DataTable"
    warning: "Always wrap in `Text(value)` if strings contain user data with brackets. Otherwise `[anything]` will be interpreted as Rich markup."
---","new_string":"---
title: DataTable Rich Markup Escaping
last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT"
audit_result: clean
read_when:
  - "adding cell values to Textual DataTable"
  - "displaying user data in DataTable cells"
  - "troubleshooting invisible text in DataTable"
tripwires:
  - action: "adding cell values to Textual DataTable"
    warning: "Always wrap in `Text(value)` if strings contain user data with brackets. Otherwise `[anything]` will be interpreted as Rich markup."
curated_in: erk-tui
---"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/textual/widget-development.md","old_string":"---

session.tool_result: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md

1→# Post-Implementation CI 2→ 3→Run CI validation after plan implementation using `make fast-ci`. 4→ 5→Load the `ci-iteration` skill for the iterative fix workflow. 6→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
session.assistant: I need to run `make fast-ci`. Let me use the devrun agent for this.

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` and report results. This is the fast CI check for the erk project.

session.tool_result:
The `make fast-ci` command has FAILED due to formatting issues found by Prettier. Here's the summary:
## Prettier Check Failed
**Code style issues found in 9 files:**
1. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/architecture.md` 2. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/async-operations.md` 3. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/commands.md` 4. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/data-layer.md` 5. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/filtering-and-views.md` 6. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/screens-and-widgets.md` 7. `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/textual-quirks.md` 8. `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` 9. `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md`
## Other Checks Status
All other checks passed successfully:
- **Ruff (Lint)**: All checks passed - **Ruff (Format)**: 1690 files already formatted - **CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md**: Standard passed, properly paired - **Type checking (ty)**: All checks passed - **Unit Tests (erk)**: 5982 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-dev)**: 136 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)**: 115 passed - **Tests (erk-mcp)**: All passed
The failure is solely due to Prettier formatting violations in 9 markdown files within the `.claude/skills/erk-tui/` and `docs/learned/` directories.
... [1 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Prettier formatting issues in the new skill files and 2 existing docs. Let me fix them.

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `npx prettier --write .claude/skills/erk-tui/ docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` and report results.

Prettier passed: 9 files processed successfully.
All markdown files have been formatted: - 7 files in `.claude/skills/erk-tui/references/` (architecture.md, async-operations.md, commands.md, data-layer.md, filtering-and-views.md, screens-and-widgets.md, textual-quirks.md) - 1 file in `.claude/skills/erk-tui/` (SKILL.md - unchanged) - 2 documentation files (github-review-decision.md, status-indicators.md) agentId: af256f7 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` and report results. All checks should pass now after prettier formatting was applied.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! All fast CI checks passed. Here's the summary:
**make fast-ci: PASSED - All checks successful**
**Results:** - **Lint**: All checks passed - **Format Check**: 1690 files already formatted - **Prettier Check**: All matched files use Prettier code style - **Markdown Check**: Passed - **Exec Reference Check**: OK - reference.md is up to date - **Type Check (ty)**: All checks passed - **Unit Tests (erk)**: 5982 passed, 2 warnings (deprecation warnings in test code, not production code) - **Unit Tests (erk-dev)**: 136 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)**: 115 passed - **Unit Tests (erk-mcp)**: Passed - **CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pairing**: All 19 files properly paired, 0 violations
All checks are now passing after the prettier formatting was applied. agentId: a0458df (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
session.assistant: All CI checks pass. Now let me complete the final steps — signal ended, clean up impl context, upload session, and submit the PR.
